meta.title: EMSE — 30-day mortality after status epilepticus (nomogram)
meta.authors: Brigo F, Turcato G, Lattanzi S, Orlandi N, Giovannini G, Zaboli A, Meletti S
meta.outcome: Individual probability of 30-day mortality after status epilepticus, from the EMSE parameters (aetiology, EEG, age, comorbidity) re-fitted into a logistic nomogram.
meta.source_note: Nomogram: Brigo, Meletti et al., Neurocrit Care 2022 (doi 10.1007/s12028-022-01548-6, PMID 35778648) — a logistic nomogram built on the EMSE parameters (Leitinger et al., Neurocrit Care 2015, doi 10.1007/s12028-014-0080-y) that gives an individual 30-day-mortality probability rather than a threshold. n=698 (202 deaths); AUC 0.830 vs 0.777 for EMSE-EACE alone.
meta.cff_info:
What this is. An individualised estimate of 30-day mortality after status epilepticus (SE). It uses the four EMSE parameters — aetiology, EEG, age and comorbidity — but re-fitted by Brigo, Meletti et al. (2022) into a nomogram that returns a continuous probability of death, instead of the original EMSE's single 64-point cut-off.

Why this version. The original EMSE only tells you “above or below the cut-off”; this nomogram gives an actual percentage for the individual patient. In the derivation cohort (698 patients) it had better discrimination than EMSE-EACE (AUC 0.830 vs 0.777) and is calibrated across the whole risk range.

How it is scored. Each of the four items contributes nomogram points; the total maps to the predicted 30-day-mortality probability. Aetiology and age dominate (anoxia and age >80 carry the most points).

Caveats. Single-centre (Modena, Italy), internally validated by bootstrap only — external validation is still needed. Lateralized (LPDs) and generalized periodic/triphasic (GPDs) and after-ictal discharges (ASIDs) were recorded together, so their EEG points are shared. The nomogram covers the aetiologies listed; for an uncommon cause not shown, pick the closest. Population-level estimate — apply clinical judgement.

source_link.label: PubMed
source_link.title: Brigo, Meletti et al., Neurocrit Care 2022 — EMSE 30-day mortality nomogram

predictors.name: Aetiology (worst single cause)
predictors.assess: The single underlying cause of the status epilepticus (nomogram points from Brigo 2022, Fig 1). Acute CNS-infection is the reference (0). 'Acute' cerebrovascular disease / brain injury = within the past 2 weeks; otherwise use the 'remote' category. For an uncommon cause not listed, pick the closest.

predictors.name: Age
predictors.assess: Patient age band (nomogram points, Brigo 2022 Fig 1). 41–50 years is the reference (0); risk rises steeply above 60.

predictors.name: Comorbidity (worst Charlson group)
predictors.assess: The patient's worst comorbidity group (Charlson-based, Brigo 2022 Fig 1). None = 0. Do not double-count the aetiology already chosen above.

options.label: Group A: MI, heart failure, PVD, cerebrovascular, dementia, chronic pulmonary, connective-tissue, ulcer, mild liver disease, diabetes
Group B: hemiplegia, moderate/severe renal disease, diabetes with end-organ damage, any tumour incl. leukaemia/lymphoma
Group C: moderate or severe liver disease

predictors.name: Worst EEG pattern
predictors.assess: The worst EEG pattern (nomogram points, Brigo 2022 Fig 1). LPDs (lateralized), GPDs (generalized periodic/triphasic) and ASIDs (after-status ictal discharges) were recorded together, so they share the same points.

model.panel_title: EMSE nomogram — 30-day mortality after status epilepticus
model.note: 30-day mortality from the Brigo/Meletti 2022 EMSE nomogram: lp = 0.284·(total nomogram points) − 4.30, mortality = 1/(1+e^−lp). The linear map (points→probability) was read from the nomogram's aligned Total-points/Mortality axes (Fig 1); point values are exact from Fig 1. Derivation n=698, AUC 0.830, bootstrap-validated (no external validation yet).
model.viz_info: Each EMSE item — aetiology, age, comorbidity, worst EEG pattern — steps the predicted 30-day mortality up from the reference-patient baseline (waterfall). The donut shows the total predicted 30-day mortality. Aetiology and age carry the most weight.

provenance.source: EMSE-nomogram point values taken verbatim from Brigo et al., Neurocrit Care 2022, Figure 1 (aetiology acute-CNS-infection 0 … anoxia 10.0; EEG none 0 / burst-suppression 2.8 / ASIDs-LPDs-GPDs 3.0; age 41–50 ref 0 … >80 7.6; comorbidity none 0 / A 2.0 / B 3.6 / C 7.0). Total-points→30-day-mortality logistic map (lp = 0.284·points − 4.30) digitized from the aligned dual axis of Fig 1 (Total points 0→~32 vs Mortality 1%→99% on a logit scale; 33.3 px/point, 117 px/logit unit). PDF in 0_inbox/papers/status_epilepticus/.
provenance.notes: REPLACES the earlier EMSE-EACE threshold build (which only gave ~8% below / ~50% above cut-off 64) with the Brigo 2022 continuous 30-day-mortality nomogram, per user request for a usable probability. type=logistic, coef 0.284 on nomogram points, intercept −4.30 (= the digitized axis map). '≤30 years' added at 0.5 (≈31–40 band; Brigo age table starts at 31–40). Comorbidity is worst-group single-select (nomogram), not the original CCI×10. Note the paper's own worked-example caption has internal typos (component scores don't sum to its stated total), so calibration relies on the printed axis, not the caption. Brigo dropped 4 rarer Leitinger aetiologies (CNS-anomalies, MS, hydrocephalus, drug overdose) — not offered here.
